name: Cruachan
description: '''Cruachan''' ({{IPA|ga|ˈkɾˠuəxən̪ˠ|pron}}) is an Irish [[folk metal]] band formed in [[Dublin]] in 1992 by multi-instrumentalist Keith Fay. With a specific focus on [[Celtic music]] and the use of [[Celtic mythology]] in its lyrics, Cruachan's style of folk metal is called [[Celtic metal]]. The band, named after the ancient capital of the kingdom of [[Connacht]], has released nine studio albums over its career, has been recognised as one of the founders of folk metal, and has been acclaimed as having "gone the greatest lengths of anyone in their attempts to expand" the genre. As of 2026, the lineup features Keith Fay on vocals, guitars, and various folk instruments; Joe Farrell on bass; David Quinn on guitar; Audrey Trainor on violin; and Sterling Paterson on drums.
